title: artocarpus incisus
intro: Artocarpus incisus, also known as the Lakshmi or Lakshmi Narayani tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia, belonging to the Moraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Artocarpus incisus include reducing fever, treating digestive issues, and supporting cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant include the leaves, which can be made into a decoction or infused in hot water to create a tea, while the bark is used to make a powder or paste for topical application.
